OIC - Gretzky Hour

Full Hockey Gear Required
Each player is required to check in at the front desk before each session and must wear their sticker receipt visible on their pants.
Full USA Hockey Certified equipment is required.
Limit 40 skaters and 4 goalies.
No outside or private coaches are allowed on the ice at any time. - Unless approved through management.

-The session is for Figure Skaters FS1 or higher.
-Priority of right of way is in the following order:
          1) Skaters in Programs
          2) Skaters in Lesson
          3) All other freestyle skaters
-Please be mindful of your surroundings


Freestyle Rules:

- All Skaters must check in at Front desk before entering Ice
- NO Virtual/Zoom Lessons
- NO Refunds, Credits, Session changes or exchanges.
- NO Waitlist for Full Sessions

Note:Class Time: 5:15pm - 5:45pmPractice/Free time: 5:45pm - 6:15pm Class curriculum:Tiny Tot 1: Proper way to fall and get-up. Marching while moving Two-foot jump in place.  There are no refunds, make ups or credits for missed classes.

Note:
Class Time: 5:15pm - 5:45pm
Practice/Free time: 5:45pm - 6:15pm

Class curriculum:
Tiny Tot 2:

  1. Beginner Two-Foot Glide
  2. Push and Glide Stroking
  3. Preparation for Snowplow Stop
  4. Dip
  5. Forward Swizzle



There are no refunds, make ups or credits for missed classes.

Note:
Class Time: 5:15pm - 5:45pm
Practice/Free time: 5:45pm - 6:15pm

Class curriculum:
Tiny Tot 3:

  1. T-Position and Push off Right & Left
  2. Backward Wiggles
  3. Two or One Foot Snowplow Stop
  4. Backward Swizzle



There are no refunds, make ups or credits for missed classes.

NOTE: Please read the description for your registered class time.
Class Time: 5:15pm - 5:45pm
Practice/Free time: 5:45pm - 6:15pm

 

Beginner 1/Pre-Alpha beginning level skating class for ages 6-17

Class curriculum:

  1. Two-foot glide
  2. One-foot glide/ right foot/ left foot
  3. Forward Swizzle
  4. Backward Wiggle
  5. Backward Swizzle
  6. Forward Pumps

There are no refunds, make ups or credits for missed classes.

Class Time: 5:15pm - 5:45pmPractice/Free time: 5:45pm - 6:15pm Prerequisite: Beginner 1/Pre-Alpha ClassClass curriculum: Forward Stroking. Forward Crossovers left over right. Forward Crossovers right over left. One foot snowplow stops. Two-foot turn There are no refunds, make ups or credits for missed classes.

PLEASE NOTE: this is not a Beginner class. Must be comfortable with basic gliding, 1 foot glides, and stopping. 

Figures are a discipline in skating that requires the skater to learn edges and turns on circles. They help the skater immensely with body alignment, edges and turns, required in all disciplines of figure skating.
All participants must be accompanied by a registered instructor.

NOTE: Please read the description for your registered class time.
Practice/Warmup Time: 5:45pm - 6:15pm
Class time: 6:15pm - 6:45pm

Intermediate 1/Gamma First Intermediate level skating class for ages 6-17

Class Curriculum:

  1. Left Forward Outside 3-Turn
  2. Right Forward Outside 3-Turn
  3. Left Forward Inside Mohawk
  4. Right Forward Inside Mohawk
  5. Hockey Stop
  6. Introduction to Backward Edges

There is No refunds, make ups or credits for missed classes.

NOTE: Please read the description for your registered class time.
Practice/Warmup Time: 5:45pm - 6:15pm
Class time: 6:15pm - 6:45pm

Intermediate 2/Delta Second Intermediate level skating class for ages 6-17

Class Curriculum:

  1. Left Forward Inside 3-Turn
  2. Right Forward Inside 3-Turn
  3. Forward Outside Edges
  4. Forward Inside Edges
  5. Lunge or Shoot the Duck
  6. One-half Flip

There is No refunds, make ups or credits for missed classes.

Class Time: 6:15pm - 6:45pm Practice/Free time: 5:45pm - 6:15pm Beginner 3/Beta Third beginning level skating class for ages 6-17Prerequisite: Beginner 2/AlphaClass Curriculum: Backward Crossovers right over left. Backward Crossovers left over right. Backward Stroking. Right T-Stop. Left T-Stop. There are no refunds, make ups or credits for missed classes.
All participants must be accompanied by a registered instructor.
All participants must be accompanied by a registered instructor.

Group instruction includes a 30 minute class and 30 minute supervised practice. Tuesday Adult Advanced Taught by experienced coaches to cater to the desire of the class. The Tuesday Adult Advanced class goes beyong the fundamental skills of adult beginner and intermediate to learn advanced turns, changes of edge, spins, jumps, and sometimes choreographed step sequences. *Please note, this is a 1 hour class split onto both sheets of ice. 6:15-6:45pm Class on OLYMPIC rink, 6:45-7:15pm Practice on NHL rink*

Group instruction includes a 30 minute class and 30 minute supervised practice, Beginner 1 (Pre-Alpha equivalent) & Adult Beginner 1 Curriculum: 1. Two foot glide 2. One-foot glide/ right foot/ left foot 3. Forward Swizzle 4. Backward Wiggle 5. Backward Swizzle 6. Forward Pumps

Group instruction includes a 30 minute class and 30 minute supervised practice. Prerequisite: Adult Beginner 1 (proficient forward & backwards pumps/c-cuts) Beginner 2 (Alpha equivalent) & Adult Beginner 2 Curriculum: 1. Forward Stroking 2. Forward Crossovers left over right (5) 3. Forward Crossovers right over left (5) 4. One foot snowplow stop 5. Two foot turn Beginner 3 (Beta equivalent) & Adult Beginner 3 Curriculum: 1. Backward Crossovers right over left (5) 2. Backward Crossovers left over right (5) 3. Backward Stroking (6) 4. Right T-Stop 5. Left T-Stop 6. Introduction to Forward Edges

Group instruction includes a 30 minute class on the Olympic rink and 30 minute supervised practice on the NHL rink. Prerequisite: Adult Beginner 2/3.Intermediate 1 (Gamma equivalent) & Adult Intermediate Curriculum: 1. Left Forward Outside 3-Turn 2. Right Forward Outside 3-Turn 3. Left Forward Inside Mohawk 4. Right Forward Inside Mohawk 5. Hockey Stop 6. Introduction to Backward Edges  7. Left Forward Inside 3-Turn 8. Right Forward Inside 3-Turn 9. Forward Outside Edges 10. Forward Inside Edges 11. Lunge or Shoot the Duck 12. One-half Flip

attributes.desc: OIC - Adult Prep for Ice Dance & More
attributes.best_description:
This class will focus on developing the skating skills of the LTS Intermediate and Advanced Adult student who seeks more expertise and strength in edge quality and turns. The curriculum will include stroking, crossovers, chasses, progressives, swing rolls and change edge as well as C-turns (Mohawks) and three turns. It will better position the student to further their exploration into advanced skating disciplines including Figures, LTS Advanced, Dance and Synchro.

Successful completion of this series will meet the prerequisite for Ice Dance One.

Prerequisite: Minimum One series of Intermediate Classes or equivalent
All participants must be accompanied by a registered instructor.
All participants must be accompanied by a registered instructor.
All participants must be accompanied by a registered instructor.

attributes.desc: Takedown - OIC - Adult Prep for Ice Dance & More

attributes.best_description: Intense Dynamic Hockey Training (IDHT) AA or interested AA players IDHT will encompass intense dynamic training sessions in a highly competitive environment focused on the development and enjoyment of high-level players. Players will be invited to participate in IDHT via selection by the coaching staff through application process.  Players interested in participating in 14U, or 16U/18U/Junior and Prep Intense Dynamic Hockey Training (IDHT) sessions must complete the IDHT Player Profile & Interest Form. Profiles will be reviewed by coaches and players will be invited to attend after reviewed. IDHT Birth Years 2026: 14U: 2013 & 2012  16U: 2011 & 2010  18U/ JR/ Prep: 2009 +
All participants must be accompanied by a registered instructor.

Division 5 - Teams should consist of mid-level players. In example: played in the ACHA (Div 2 or Div 3); O35 (SJ); O40 (OAK). In addition, these teams could consist of former youth players (A or BB). 18-Game regular season.
